DISCOVERY 301, A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF RYPOTUS LIMITED'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF A WRIT OF ATTCHMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, A TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER (AS TO LEIGH E. SPRAGUE) IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 485.010 (B)(1) WHICH IS LIMITED FOR 60 DAYS. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE EVIDENCE SUPPORTS VALID SUBSTITUTED SERVICE BUT ALSO RECOGNIZES THERE ARE ISSUES WITH RESPECT TO THIS. THE TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER IS STILL IN EFFECT UNTIL THE NEW ORDER IS SIGNED BY THE COURT. MR. SPRAGUE MAY CONTINUE TO TRANSFER FUNDS DIRECTLY TO HIS ATTORNEY FOR FEES EXPENDED IN GOOD FAITH FOR THE DEFENSE OF THIS LITIGATION. MR. MARTIN TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J.
